sizing_rule.note: Ralph Vince optimal f method: size positions by calculating HPR = 1 + f*(-Trade/Biggest Loss). Optimal f derived from win probability p using formula ((2*p)-1)/2 or ((2+l)*0.5-l)/2. Historical biggest loss serves as denominator reference for risk scaling. Specific f value must be calculated from historical trade data before application.

source_rule_text: Size positions using optimal f by calculating HPR (Holding Period Return) with the formula HPR = 1 + f*(-Trade/Biggest Loss), where f represents the fraction of capital to risk based on historical biggest loss.
